title: DIY Bathtub Tray
summary: This DIY bathtub tray is an easy beginner woodworking project and a great addition to any bathroom.
description: This DIY bathtub tray is an easy beginner woodworking project and a great addition to any bathroom. Relax and pamper yourself! This can be customized according to the width of your bathtub.

steps.title: Cut Your Wood
steps.body: Cut your wood to the specified dimensions. This tray is cut to 38" long to fit this specific bathtub, but make sure to measure and cut it to the right length to fit yours.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Drill 2 pocket holes along one of the long sides of each of your 2 1x2 pieces.

steps.title: Sand Your Wood
steps.body: Use a sander to sand all of your wood smooth and get rid of any jagged edges.

steps.title: Attach Legs
steps.body: Use 1 1/4" pocket hole screws to attach your legs to your tray, placing them approximately 6 inches in from each end (these can be adjusted depending on the width of your bathtub edge). The purpose of these "legs" is to keep the tray from sliding back and forth or falling in the tub.

steps.title: Stain and Seal
steps.body: Stain your tray in the color of your choice. I used a Provincial colored stain for this one. Since this will be used around water and needs to be protected, I sealed it with a spar urethane.

steps.title: Add Handles
steps.body: Drill holes and add your handles. I placed mine about 2" in from each end, and used 5 1/2" long handles.
